name: Black Pepper
description: A pungent climbing vine spice. The primary alkaloid Piperine serves as a potent enhancer of compound bioavailability by inhibiting metabolic enzymes (glucuronidation) in the gut and liver, boosting nutrient and herbal absorption.
preparation: 0.5g to 1.5g of freshly ground black pepper taken concurrently with target compounds or dietary supplements.
safetyAlert: Extremely safe at typical dietary doses. High therapeutic piperine intake may increase absorption of co-administered pharmaceuticals, requiring clinical observation.
interactions: Inhibits hepatic glucuronidation, CYP3A4, and P-glycoprotein, significantly boosting absorption and bioavailability of Curcumin and multiple clinical medications.
sideEffects: Mild gastric warming, minor acid reflux, mucosal irritation in massive doses.
contraindications: Active bleeding gastrointestinal ulcers, severe esophageal reflux, or hyperacidity.
countyStorageAdvisory: Mombasa: Store in air-tight, vacuum-sealed glass jars to prevent high coastal humidity from causing mold or clumping. Lodwar: Store in a shaded, ventilated container to prevent essential oil evaporation from high arid temperatures.
dosingRules: Children under 2: Not advised. Children 2-5: Micro-pinch (approx 0.05g) max. Adults: 0.5g - 1g (approx 1/4 teaspoon) ground powder per dose concurrently with main compound.
